text: The deal also includes at least 5 GW of compute capacity for Anthropic.

$GOOGL is reportedly investing another $10B into Anthropic at a $350B valuation.

Google may invest up to $30B more if Anthropic hits certain performance targets.
$NVDA is back above $5 Trillion because the AI infrastructure cycle still has not shown signs of slowing.

$AMZN Zoox is now testing robotaxi rides to and from Las Vegas International Airport.

The service is employee-only for now with a public rollout expected in the relatively near future.

Great to be featured in Reuters today discussing what investors need to know before buying into a potential ~$2T SpaceX IPO.

If you view SpaceX as a space & connectivity company then the valuation looks ambitious but if you view it as an emerging AI infrastructure platform built around Starlink, xAI, Colossus & potentially Cursor then the valuation starts to make more sense.
I bought these $MU options a month ago when the market sold off on the ridiculous $GOOGL TurboQuant FUD and they’re now up +325%:
$MU JUST BROKE ABOVE $500 FOR FIRST TIME EVER

AI bears checking the portfolio after passing on $INTC under $20, $MU under $100, $NVDA near $100, $AVGO under $180 and $AMD under $200.

Apparently the “bubble” had some lovely entry points over the past year.
Even Burry looked at $MSFT near an 8-year low valuation and decided he’d have some of that.

Analysts were clearly offsides on how disruptive the CPU bottleneck is becoming in this AI cycle and didn't see $INTC as the primary beneficiary at current supply levels.

I’d expect a flood of upgrades in the coming weeks.

AI startups are struggling to access $NVDA GPUs as cloud providers like $MSFT, $AMZN, $GOOGL & $CRWV reserve more capacity for internal teams & larger AI customers.

The shortage is driving months-long wait times, higher prices and longer contract commitments with Azure delays reportedly expected to last through the end of 2026.

$GOOGL TPU shift is now pulling demand across the full AI compute stack from silicon & packaging to optics, power & memory:

• CPU Architecture: $ARM
• Optical Circuit Switch: $COHR, $LITE, $FN
• Memory: $MU, $SNDK, Samsung, SK Hynix
• Server Power & Analog: $MPWR, $AEIS, $VICR
• Optical & Interconnect: $AAOI, $MRVL, $CRDO
• Thermal & Power Management: $VRT, $MOD, $NVT
• High-Density Interconnect Cables: $APH, $TEL, $GLW
• ASIC Design, Manufacturing & Packaging: $AVGO, $TSM, $AMKR

$META is a ~12% position in my family portfolio
$AMZN is a ~9% position in my family portfolio
$ARM is a ~3% position in my family portfolio

The $META + $AMZN Graviton deal validates three things at once: 

1. The CPU bottleneck is VERY  real
2. $ARM based CPUs are gaining traction in AI data centers
3. AWS’s custom silicon moat becoming more important as AI shifts from training to inference + agents

📺 BUY THE DIP IS BACK — SOME STRONG NAMES TO CONSIDER

In a strong uptrend, pullbacks are once again becoming opportunities as leaders hold key levels and momentum remains intact. 

Some strong names to consider:

– $MU is the focus after a powerful move, with a dip buy setup above $471
– $NVDA continues to tighten along the 8-day.
– Mega caps like $AMZN and $GOOGL are forming bullish flags, offering both support-based entries and breakout potential. 

Use a disciplined approach here: build positions in tiers, buy controlled dips, and add on confirmation rather than chase strength.

With earnings approaching, the market is offering multiple high-quality setups for active traders who remain patient and respect key levels.
